User shared screenshot of a success fee carbon model (price/ton × success fee % × tons, risk-adjusted 2026–2030) and Excel of registered MSW WTE plants. Asked if MSW data could be used to build a similar model.
Read MSW Excel file, searched for VCS ACM0022 market pricing, built full MSW Success Fee Model on Sheet1 matching screenshot structure.

10 projects from MSW file loaded. Carbon price set to $6/ton (from Ecosystem Marketplace 2025 avg for reduction credits; highlighted yellow). Fee % and prob adj default to 10% and 50% (blue inputs). Crediting periods used to zero out inactive years. Doxaco/Seraphin set to 0 (missing data). Legend, crediting period ref table, and color coding added.
MSW Success Fee Model complete on Sheet1. User should update blue input cells (D:F columns) with actual project-specific prices, fees, and probabilities from group calls.
User uploaded Allotrope-ViOT_DOXACO Project_Calculation.xlsx as additional context for the Doxaco WTE project row in the MSW model.

Read and analyzed Doxaco calculation file (3 sheets: ACM0022, Survey, Calculation). Extracted per-year net ER figures. Updated Doxaco row in Sheet1 model with findings. Added key findings callout box.

Critical finding: Doxaco uses ACM0022 decay model where net ER = avoided landfill methane (grows exponentially) minus incineration PE (constant ~78k/yr). Net ER is NEGATIVE in Years 1-5. With 2026 start, all years 2026-2030 are negative. Total 10-yr VCUs = 367,754 tCO2e. 10-yr average = 36,775/yr (matches original MSW file). Year-by-year ER: Yr1:-44k, Yr2:-40k, Yr3:-33k, Yr4:-24k, Yr5:-10k, Yr6:+10k, Yr7:+39k, Yr8:+82k, Yr9:+146k, Yr10:+240k.

Doxaco row updated in Sheet1 with $0 fee revenue for 2026-2030 (all negative net ER assuming 2026 start). Cell notes document per-year ER values. Key finding callout box added at bottom of sheet. User should confirm actual project start date with project team.
User asked for assessment of extending model to 2035 and whether we have enough data.

Analyzed all 10 projects for 2031-2035 data coverage. Built 2031-2035 extension columns (M-Q) with confirmed data only. Added green header band labeling extended period. Added R column for full 2026-2035 total.

Coverage: Liuzhou & Shunping active through May 2032 (confirmed). Qixian active through Dec 2031. Doxaco turns positive in 2031 (Yr6 = +10,174 rising to +239,745 by 2035, from calc file). 7 other projects expired pre-2031 with no renewal data — set to 0. Seraphin still no data. Decision: conservative approach (no assumed renewals) per user preference.

2031-2035 columns added to Sheet1. Extended total column R added. Key limitation: most value comes from Doxaco in 2031-2035 as other projects expire. Model clearly distinguishes confirmed vs. expired periods.

User shared screenshot of a success fee model (per-client price/ton, fee%, probability, yearly tons 2026-2030) and asked to review the Model sheet against it. Wants to know if similar model can be built for MADA and VN projects, and where to find estimates from prior group call presentations.
Reviewed Model sheet structure, formulas, and data against screenshot. Compared column layout, formula logic, per-project assumptions, and identified structural gaps.

Model sheet matches screenshot framework (price × fee% × probability × tons per year). Key differences: screenshot has per-client unique assumptions ($3-$150/ton, 3-17% fee, 25-90% probability) while Model uses uniform $6/ton, 10% fee, 50% probability. Screenshot clients appear to be CDR/carbon removal projects, not MSW WTE. VN projects (Soc Son, Doxaco, Seraphin) are present but Doxaco shows $0 revenue 2026-2030 (negative net ER). No MADA projects exist in workbook.

Model structure is sound and matches screenshot format. To fulfill request: (1) MADA project data is completely missing — user needs to source estimates from group call presentations. (2) Per-project pricing/fee/probability differentiation needed to match screenshot approach. (3) Seraphin still has no ER data. User should provide project-specific inputs to flesh out model.
